prevalence.source: IHME GBD / WHO (anxiety disorders) + psychiatric epidemiology syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; increased recognition in young adults

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11: marked fear/anxiety in social situations with avoidance and impairment for ≥6 months

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often underdiagnosed; recognized during broader anxiety/depression assessments in APS/CAPS
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: CBT (exposure-based) and SSRIs; group therapy when available
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited CBT access and stigma delaying care
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: SUS availability of SSRIs and psychosocial care pathways in CAPS
